question: Find the compound interest on Rs8,000 at 12% per annum for 3 years 4 months, compounded annually.
The compound interest on a sum at 18 % p.a. for 1 years, compounded yearly, is Rs2 862. Find the principal.
If the compound interest on a certain sum at 16 per annum for 3 years is Rs1270, find the simple interest on the same sum at the same rate and for the same period.
A principal of Rs1,00,000 is invested at 10 % per annum compound interest, compounded annually. After how many years will the amount grow to Rs1,33,100?
Find the compound interest on Rs10,000 at 10% per annum for 2 years 6 months, compounded annually.
A certain amount invested at compound interest of 12% per annum, compounded annually, amounts to Rs3,136 in 2 years. What is 140% of the amount invested?
A certain amount grows to Rs6400 in 2 years and Rs7040 in 3 years. Determine the interest rate.
In how many years will Rs50,000 become Rs66,550 at 10% compound interest per annum?
Find the compound interest on Rs7,000 at 9% p.a. for 1 year 8 months, compounded annually.
If the amount at the end of the 2nd year and 3rd year on a certain principal at compound interest is Rs12,000 and Rs13,200 respectively, find the rate of interest per annum.
A sum becomes Rs12100 in 2 years at 10% Compound Interest annually. Find the principal.

A TV set is available for Rs19,650 in cash or for Rs3,100 as a cash down payment and three equal annual instalments. If the shopkeeper charges interest at the rate of 10% per annum compounded annually, calculate the amount of each instalment.
A sum becomes 4 times its original value in 6 years under compound interest. In how many years will it become 16 times of the original amount?
If Rs5000 amounts to Rs6050 over 2 years with compound interest, what is the annual interest rate?
What is the compound interest on a sum of Rs. 12,500 at 15% p.a. in 2 years, if the interest is compounded 8-monthly?
A certain amount increases to five times its initial value after 10 years of compounding interest. How many years will it take for it to grow to twenty-five times its original amount?
How much will the compound interest on Rs. 16000 at 20% per annum for 9 months if it is compounded quarterly?
What is the compound interest on Rs. 8000 for 2 year at 10% per annum?
A vendor lends 74,000 rupees at a rate of 12% compound interest per annum, compounded annually. Find the interest for the 3rd year.
A sum of money invested at compound interest amounts to twice itself in 4 years. In how many years will it become 8 times itself?
A sum of money becomes three times itself in 3 years at compound interest. What is the rate of interest?
What will be the compound interest on a sum of Rs. 25,000 after 3 years at the rate of 10% p.a.?
What will be the amount to be paid at the end of 5 years on Rs. 4500 at 10% per annum compounded annually?
The compound interest on a certain sum for 3 years at 20% per annum is Rs864. Find the simple interest on the same sum at the same rate and period.
A sum of money doubles itself at compound interest in 15 years. In how many years will it become eight times?
Vidhya lent Rs. 6000 to Kavya for 3 years at the rate of 4% per annum compound interest. Calculate the amount that Vidhya will get after 3 years.
A man got 10% of his investment as profit. If he invested Rs. 5000, and the profit was reinvested, what will be the total value after the second year with the same profit rate?
Calculate the compound interest on Rs. 5000 for 2 years at 20% per annum when compounded half yearly.
A certain sum amounts to Rs. 1,600 in two years and to Rs. 2,000 in three years at compound interest when compounded annually. Find the rate of interest.
Albert invested an amount of Rs. 6000 in a fixed deposit scheme for 3 years at a compound interest rate of 5% p.a. How much will Albert get on maturity of the fixed deposit?
If the amount is Rs. 600 and the Principal is Rs. 450, which is compounded yearly for 1 year, calculate the rate of interest.
At what rate of interest per annum will a sum of Rs15,625 amount to Rs19,683 in 1 year 6 months, if the interest is compounded half-yearly?
The difference between the CI and SI on a sum for 2 years at 15% p.a. is Rs. 360. What is the principal? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 3]

An amount of Rs. 2,00,000 is invested at 10% per annum compound interest. After 1 year, the investor withdraws 20,000 from the balance. What will be the total amount remaining in the account at the end of the 2nd year? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 3]
A sum becomes 4 times its original value in 6 years under compound interest. In how many years will it become 16 times of the original amount? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 13 Nov Shift 1]
The compound interest on Rs8,000 for 1 year 6 months at 20% p.a., compounded half- yearly, is:
A man deposits Rs1,50,000 at a CI rate of 10% p.a. After the first year's interest is credited, he withdraws 50% of the interest earned. What is his account balance at the end of the second year?
The amount at the end of the 2nd and 3rd years on a certain principal at CI is Rs4,232 and Rs4,655.20 respectively. Find the rate of interest per annum.
A machine is purchased for Rs80,000. Its value depreciates at the rate of 10% per year. What will be its value after 3 years?
The difference between the compound interest and simple interest on a sum for 2 years at 10% p.a. is Rs15. What is the sum?
The amount at the end of the 2nd and 3rd years on a certain principal at CI is Rs9,680 and Rs10,648 respectively. Find the principal.

A sum is invested at compound interest (compounded annually) at a certain rate. The interest earned in the 1st year is Rs. 2,000, and in the 2nd year it is Rs. 2,400. Find 50% of the sum invested. [SSC CHSL Tier 1, 15 Nov 2025, Shift-1]

Rs. 12,000 is invested at the rate of 8% p.a. for 4 years at simple interest in scheme 'A'. The amount received from scheme 'A' is then invested for 2 years at rate of 5% p.a. compound annually in scheme 'B'. Find the amount received from scheme 'B'.

A person invested Rs. 18,000 each in two different schemes for 2 years. One scheme offered 15% p.a. simple interest and the other offered 15% p.a. compound interest, compounded annually. Find the difference between the interest earned from both schemes.
If Rs. 60,000 is invested at a compound interest of 20% p.a. for 18 months, then find the interest earned given that the sum is compounded every 6 months.

A savings scheme offers compound interest annually. A woman deposits Rs10,000. At the end of the first year, the interest rate is 10%. In the second year, due to inflation, the interest rate rises to 12.5%, and in the third year, it drops to 8%. Calculate the total interest she earned at the end of 3 years.

An amount of Rs. 'z' is invested in two different schemes—one offers simple interest at 13% p.a. for 4 years, and the other offers compound interest at 12% p.a. compounded annually for 2 years. If the total amount from the simple interest scheme exceeds the compound interest amount by Rs. 78, then find the value of z.

A sum of Rs. 7,500 is invested in a scheme offering r% compound interest for 2 years. Find the value of 'r' if the difference in the simple interest and compound interest for the same amount at the same rate after 2 years is Rs. 108.

Gaurav invested Rs. 'x' in a scheme offering 10% compound interest for three years. Prem invested Rs. 18,000 in a scheme offering 15% simple interest for four years. If the interest earned by Gaurav is the interest earned by Prem less '2x - 1,200' rupees, then find the interest earned by Gaurav.
A sum of money becomes 16 times in 2 years. What is the annual compound interest rate?
A certain sum, when invested for 2 years at 12% per annum compound interest, compounded annually, amounts to Rs. 39,200. Find the sum invested (in Rs.).

A sum of Rs. 6,400 amounts to Rs. 8,100 in 8 years at a certain rate of interest, compounded annually. Find the amount received after 4 years when Rs. 2,240 are invested at same rate of compound interest (compounded annually).
A sum becomes Rs12100 in 2 years at 10% Compound Interest annually. Find the principal.
A sum is being lent at 10 percent per annum compound interest (compounding annually). What is the ratio of amount at the end of first year to the amount at the end of second year?

The ratio of the interest received when Rs. (x + 500) is invested at 32% p.a. simple interest for 4 years to the interest received when Rs. (x − 500) is invested at 20% p.a. compound interest, compounded annually for 2 years, is 64:11. Find the value of 'x'.

A man invested Rs. 30,000 each in two separate schemes at 10% p.a. for 4 years. One scheme gives simple interest, while the other scheme gives compound interest, compounded annually. Find the difference between the total interest earned from both.
The compound interest on a sum at 18% p.a. for 1 1/2 years, compounded yearly, is Rs2 862. Find the principal.
The compound interest on a certain sum at 20% p.a. for 2 1/2 years, compounded yearly, is Rs2920. Find the principal.

The amount received on a certain sum at the same annual simple interest rate after 3 years and 5 years at compound interest (compounding annually) is Rs. 5324 and Rs. 6442.04 respectively. What is that sum?

A father deposited Rs60,000 in a compound interest scheme for his son's business when his son was 20 years old. The scheme offers 15% compound interest per annum compounded annually. What will be the total amount his son will receive when he turns 24?

Meena took a loan of Rs. 8,000 at 10% compound interest per annum for 3 years. She repaid Rs. 4,000 at the end of the 1st year and the rest at the end of the 3rd year. Find the total interest paid by Meena.
An amount of Rs8,000 is invested at 10% compound interest. What will be the amount after 3 years?
A sum of money is invested at a compound interest rate of 8% p.a. for the first year and 10% p.a. for the second year. If the total interest earned is Rs1,872, what was the original principal?

Ravi invested Rs. 25,000 in two schemes for 3 years. Both schemes offer 12% p.a. interest. One gives simple interest and the other gives compound interest (compounded annually). Find the difference between the two interests earned for these 3 years.
A sum of Rs. 6250 amounts to Rs. 7290 in 2 years at compound interest. Find the rate per annum.

The amount received on a certain sum at the same annual rate of interest after 3 years and 5 years on compound interest (compounding annually) is Rs. 10648 and Rs. 12884.08 respectively. What is that sum?
What sum will amount to Rs. 23958 in 3 years at the rate of 10 percent per annum at compound interest (compounding annually)?
A sum is being lent at 12.5 percent per annum compound interest (compounding annually). What is the ratio of amount at the end of first year to the amount at the end of second year?
Find CI on Rs1000 for 3 years at 10% p.a.?
Rs. 4000 is invested in a scheme of compound interest (compounding annually) at the annual rate of 15 percent. What will be the difference between compound interest of second year and third year?
Rs. 6000 is invested in a scheme of compound interest (compounding annually) at the annual rate of 20 percent. What will be the difference between compound interest of second year and third year?
Find the CI on Rs5000 at 8% p.a. for 1 year.

The amount received on a certain sum at the same annual rate of interest after 3 years and 5 years on compound interest (compounding annually) is Rs. 15972 and Rs. 19326.12 respectively. What is that sum?
CI on Rs2000 for 1 year at 10% p.a. is:
An amount is said to double in 5 years with compound interest. How many years will it take for the amount to grow to 8 times its original value? [SSC CHSL 2025 Tier 1, 12 Nov Shift 2]
A sum of money is invested at compound interest. The amount after 2 years is Rs15,972 and after 3 years it becomes Rs17,888.64. What is the rate of interest per annum?
A sum of money invested at compound interest amounts to Rs. 1,000 in 3 years and 1,200 in 4 years. Find the rate of interest.
A sum of Rs. 22,500 is invested at 4% per annum compound interest. What will be the amount after 2 years?
If Roshan lent Rs. 6,000 at 9% per annum compound interest, what will be the amount needs to pay after 2 years?
A startup founder puts Rs. 8,000 into a venture. After 2 years, the investment grows to Rs. 8,816 with interest compounded once every year. What is the annual interest rate applied?

A corporate travel fund of Rs. 12,000 was set aside by a logistics company for international staff trips. The amount was invested at compound interest. The team noticed that the fund doubled to Rs. 24,000 in 5 years. If they don't withdraw anything, how much will the fund grow to after 20 years?
The population of a town increases by 2% per year. If the present population is 49,000, find the population after 2 years.
Ramesh invests Rs. 68,000 at 20% p.a. compound interest for 2 years. He then adds Rs. 16,000 more and invests the total for 1 more year at same rate. Find total amount after 3 years.
A sum of Rs. 40,000 becomes Rs. 56,244 in 2 years at compound interest compounded annually. What is the rate of interest?
Ramesh invested Rs. 50,000 at an annual compound interest rate of 5%. What will be the compound interest after 3 years?
Find the compound interest on a principal of Rs. 85,000 for a term of 1.5 years at a nominal annual rate of 24%, compounded on a half-yearly basis.
A sum of money becomes after two years and after  years on compound interest. The sum is:
The simple interest and compound interest that can be earned in two years at the same rate is and respectively. What is the rate of interest per annum?
The population increases by 13% annually. If the current population is 43,000, what will it be after 3 years? (in approx.)
The difference between the compound interest and simple interest on Rs18,000 for 2 years is Rs405. What is the rate of interest per annum?
source: Selection Post Phase XIV (Graduate), 02 Aug 2025, Shift 3
